rationale.R1.2: The passage advances several causal/inferential claims — disagreement as a novelty flag, ensemble gain as a consequence of representational diversity — whose logical validity is reviewable if a scientific reading is intended.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims (63% disagreement-to-novelty rate; macro-F1 0.81; 4–7 F1 point margins over individual classifiers; rare-class lift from 0.30 to 0.55) must be checked against the described experimental setup and evaluation protocol if accuracy review is intended.
rationale.R1.6: The open-ended 'take a look' phrasing commonly signals a prose-quality request; sentence clarity, paragraph transitions, and expository flow across the excerpt are all squarely in scope under that reading.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary goal feedback on writing quality and prose clarity, rather than on scientific accuracy, logical soundness, or evidence-claim alignment?
